af_score_components.notes: Fair-code workflow automation platform with native AI/LangChain capabilities and 400+ integrations. n8n itself is NOT an MCP server (has_mcp_server=false) — use n8n-mcp or n8n-workflow-builder to expose n8n to AI agents. Webhooks and REST API documented. Auth includes per-workflow API keys and individual node credentials per integration — significant complexity for agents integrating many services. Enterprise tier adds SSO (SAML/OIDC). Fair-code license restricts commercial redistribution. Self-hosting requires infrastructure overhead.

security_score_components.security_notes: n8n Cloud enforces HTTPS; self-hosted requires TLS configuration. Enterprise SSO (SAML/OIDC) for strong identity management. Per-node credential storage in n8n's secure credential store — documented best practice: never pass credentials as workflow parameters. Enterprise advanced permissions provide workflow-level access control. Large 400+ integration surface area increases dependency hygiene concerns. Fair-code license with active commercial development.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: n8n Cloud has SLA; self-hosted is fully operator-managed with significant infrastructure overhead. Active versioned releases. Large platform with many integrations means some ecosystem churn. Partial idempotency. Retry guidance documented. Workflow execution monitoring available. AI agent workflows require LangChain primitives knowledge in addition to n8n concepts.

af_score_components.notes: n8n's REST API with official MCP server (n8n-io/n8n-mcp) for managing workflows, executions, and credentials. OpenAPI spec available at docs.n8n.io. MCP server quality 72/100. API key (X-N8N-API-KEY header) = full admin access — no scoped tokens. Self-hosted requires public URL for external webhook triggers. Webhook vs API-trigger workflows have different semantics. n8n handles credentials securely — documented to never pass credentials as workflow parameters. Cursor pagination with retry guidance.

security_score_components.security_notes: n8n Cloud enforces HTTPS; self-hosted requires TLS; SOC2 and GDPR compliant. API key = full admin access with no scope limitation — a concern for least privilege. Credential store for node credentials is secure and documented. Enterprise adds SSO. TypeScript codebase with active fair-code development. API key via env var (X-N8N-API-KEY header) is the recommended pattern.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: n8n Cloud has SLA (SOC2, GDPR); self-hosted operator responsibility. Versioned API with OpenAPI spec. Active development may introduce breaking changes. No idempotency support — duplicate API calls may create duplicate workflow executions. Cursor pagination available. Retry guidance documented. Webhook workflow vs API-trigger workflow require careful design for reliable agent integration.

af_score_components.notes: Community MCP server giving AI assistants comprehensive access to n8n's 1,084 node docs, 2,709 workflow templates, validation, and live instance management. MCP server quality 80/100. CRITICAL WARNING: Project explicitly warns NEVER to edit production workflows directly with AI — always use dev/staging. MCP_MODE=stdio required for local npx usage. Hosted free tier: 100 calls/day, easily exhausted during workflow iteration. 63.6% operation coverage means some nodes have incomplete schema data. Telemetry enabled by default (opt-out available).

security_score_components.security_notes: Hosted dashboard.n8n-mcp.com uses HTTPS; local self-hosted no TLS requirement. N8N_API_KEY grants full n8n admin access — no scope restriction on what the MCP server can do. Telemetry enabled by default, sending usage data that must be explicitly disabled. Community project (not official n8n-io) with moderate dependency hygiene. Env vars for N8N_API_URL and N8N_API_KEY are the correct pattern.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local self-hosted mode has no SLA; hosted dashboard service has limited transparency. Community project with active but non-guaranteed maintenance. 63.6% operation coverage means incomplete schema for complex workflows. Free tier 100 calls/day easily exhausted during iteration sessions. No retry guidance or idempotency documentation.

af_score_components.notes: Community MCP server for full n8n instance lifecycle management via 15 tools (CRUD, activate/deactivate, execute, monitor). MCP server quality 72/100. CRITICAL: Operates directly on live n8n instance — NO sandbox mode, mistakes affect real production workflows. Activate/deactivate tools can immediately disrupt running automations. Security audit tool generates reports only (no remediation). N8N_HOST + N8N_API_KEY env vars = full admin access. Error message quality 55/100. Smithery.ai hosted option may have different data handling than self-hosted.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends on target n8n instance configuration — not enforced by the MCP server itself. N8N_API_KEY grants full admin access to the live n8n instance with no scope restriction. Ability to activate/deactivate all workflows is a high-risk capability for agents. Community TypeScript project with limited security review. Smithery.ai hosted option introduces third-party data handling considerations. Env vars for credentials are the correct pattern.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Reliability depends entirely on the live n8n instance it manages — no sandbox safety net. Community project with active but uncertain long-term maintenance. No idempotency support. Activate/deactivate operations can immediately break production automations. No retry guidance. Offset pagination. Limited error recovery for failed workflow modifications.

af_score_components.notes: Domain registrar XML/REST API with no MCP server (has_mcp_server=false). XML response format requires additional parsing vs JSON. IP whitelist is a critical constraint — agents must run from static IPs (no dynamic cloud runners). API activation requires minimum account balance or domain count. Rate limit of 20 calls/minute is very restrictive for bulk domain operations. Sandbox at api.sandbox.namecheap.com uses different endpoint. DNS management slower to propagate than Cloudflare-based DNS. API key + username passed as query params (appears in server logs).

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced; ICANN and GDPR compliant. IP whitelist is a strong positive security control limiting attack surface. API key + username as query parameters is not ideal (can appear in server logs); HTTPS protects transport but URL logging is a concern. No fine-grained scopes — API key = full account access. Mature registrar with ICANN compliance requirements. Separate sandbox environment for testing is a good practice.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Commercial registrar with ICANN-required operational standards. Legacy XML API is stable but not modernized. Conservative API changes given domain management criticality. 20 calls/minute rate limit severely constrains agents doing bulk operations. No retry guidance documented. Partial idempotency. DNS propagation lag is an inherent reliability factor for DNS management workflows.

af_score_components.notes: Graph database with community MCP servers from Neo4j Labs (not production-supported; breaking changes possible). Bolt protocol is the primary query interface; HTTP API supports Cypher via transactions. LLM-generated Cypher frequently gets relationship direction wrong — Neo4j is directional (wrong direction = empty results silently). MERGE without correct property matching creates duplicate nodes silently. Runaway graph traversals without depth limit can exhaust memory. APOC plugin required for schema introspection (not on Aura Free). HTTP transactions auto-rollback after 60s of inactivity. Community Edition: single database per instance.

security_score_components.security_notes: neo4j+s:// URI scheme for TLS-encrypted connections on Aura. Self-hosted TLS configurable. Enterprise RBAC with database-level and graph-element-level privileges is excellent scope granularity. LDAP, Kerberos, SSO on Enterprise Edition. SOC2, GDPR, ISO27001 compliant. Username/password via Bolt connection string — should use env vars. Community Edition has limited role support compared to Enterprise.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Neo4j Aura has SLA; Community self-hosted is operator responsibility. Mature graph database with stable Cypher language. Long track record. Partial idempotency — MERGE can silently create duplicates without exact property matching. Skip/limit pagination. Runaway queries without depth limit are a reliability risk. HTTP transaction 60s timeout requires active keepalive. No retry guidance documented.

af_score_components.notes: Serverless PostgreSQL with outstanding MCP server (quality 92/100 — best-in-class for databases). 25+ MCP tools covering SQL, schema migration on temporary branches, query analysis, and cluster management. Three API key types (personal, org, project-scoped) with OAuth2 option. Rate limit documented: 700 req/min, 40 req/sec burst. OpenAPI spec. Scale-to-zero cold start 100-500ms — configure min_cu=0.25 for always-warm production. Async management operations return operation IDs for polling. MCP read-only mode doesn't fully prevent writes (documented limitation — run_sql can still execute DML). Database branching for zero-risk migrations is a unique agent superpower.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced; SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA, ISO27001 compliant. Three API key types enabling least-privilege: project-scoped keys restrict agent access to a single project. OAuth2 for MCP authentication. API keys shown only once at creation (no recovery — must regenerate). Bearer token via Authorization header. Excellent security posture for database-as-a-service. MCP read-only mode limitation (run_sql still allows DML) is a documented gap that needs awareness.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Commercial SaaS with SLA; SOC2 compliant. PostgreSQL compatibility guarantees inherent API stability. Versioned Management API with OpenAPI spec. Retry guidance documented. Scale-to-zero cold start adds 100-500ms latency after idle period. Async management operations must be polled before using created resources. Database branching provides excellent rollback capability. Branch compute costs accumulate if not managed. Copy-on-write branches diverge storage cost with write workloads.

af_score_components.notes: Netlify's REST API for managing sites, builds, functions, and DNS with no MCP server (has_mcp_server=false). Personal access tokens are the simplest auth method for agents (full account access). OpenAPI spec available but may have gaps. Build minute limits on free tier can be exhausted by agents triggering frequent builds. Environment variable updates require a new deploy to take effect. No idempotency. Deploy previews and production deploys have different URL patterns requiring careful handling. Page pagination. No retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: Commercial SaaS with HTTPS enforced; SOC2, GDPR compliant. Personal access tokens = full account access (no scope limitation noted as concern in agent data). OAuth2 for multi-user apps. No fine-grained scope control for agents — full account access by default. Webhook signing for incoming webhook verification. SOC2 compliance with enterprise customers. Token via env vars as standard pattern.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Enterprise CDN platform with SLA. Stable REST API with OpenAPI spec. Backward compatibility maintained for platform customers. No idempotency support — duplicate deploy triggers create duplicate deployments. Page pagination. No retry guidance documented. Build minute limits constrain agent frequency. Environment variable updates require redeploy — agents must account for propagation delay.

af_score_components.notes: Oracle NetSuite ERP REST and SOAP APIs with no MCP server (has_mcp_server=false). Documentation quality rated 62/100 — complex ERP-specific terminology. OAuth 1.0a (TBA) is complex to implement; OAuth 2.0 preferred on newer versions. Account-specific domain required (ACCOUNT_ID.suitetalk.api.netsuite.com). NetSuite admin required for setup. Concurrency limits can reject requests during peak hours. REST API and SOAP API (SuiteTalk) have different capabilities. SuiteScript (JavaScript embedded in NetSuite) often easier for complex operations. Saved Searches require NetSuite admin to set up. ERP domain knowledge required.

security_score_components.security_notes: Oracle enterprise with HTTPS enforced; SOC1, SOC2, ISO27001, HIPAA, GDPR compliant. OAuth 2.0 (preferred) or OAuth 1.0a TBA. NetSuite role-based permissions provide ERP-level access control. Admin consent required for setup. Account-specific domain prevents cross-account credential confusion. OAuth tokens managed via NetSuite credential system. Oracle enterprise security posture is strong.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Oracle NetSuite enterprise SLA (HIPAA, SOC2). Legacy ERP API — stable but features lag modern APIs. Conservative breaking changes given enterprise ERP context. Concurrency limits reject requests during peak usage requiring backoff. No retry guidance documented. Partial idempotency. Slow API responses are inherent to ERP systems. Complex domain knowledge (NetSuite record types and field names) is required for agents to operate correctly.

af_score_components.notes: Email verification service with no MCP server (has_mcp_server=false). Simple single-purpose API for validating email addresses individually or in bulk. Good documentation. Simple API key auth. Bulk job processing is async — agents must poll with job_id for completion. Webhook for bulk completion requires HTTPS callback URL. Results use numeric codes (0-4) with text labels — agents should use text labels not assume numeric mapping. Catch-all domains return 'unknown' — validation is impossible for catch-all servers. Credits consumed on 'unknown' results — unexpected credit consumption from legitimate validations.

security_score_components.security_notes: Commercial SaaS with HTTPS enforced; GDPR compliant. Single API key per account — no scope limitation. API key can be sent as query param or header (header preferred for security — avoid URL logging). No fine-grained scope control. Commercial email validation service with GDPR compliance for personal email data handling. Standard env var pattern for API key storage.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Commercial SaaS with implied SLA from GDPR-compliant operations. Mature, single-purpose API with high stability. Full idempotency for read/validation operations. Async bulk jobs with job_id polling are reliable if implemented correctly. No retry guidance documented. Credits consumed on 'unknown' results can cause unexpected quota consumption in large list validation workflows.
